title: Hedera (HBAR) Price Prediction 2026-2027
content: The market performance of Hedera (HBAR) throughout the past 12 months illustrates the volatile nature of the current Layer 1 landscape. With a 1-year high of $0.290755 and a low of $0.078421, the asset has displayed significant fluctuations alongside the broader crypto sector. As of {{LIVE_DATE}}, the asset sits at {{LIVE_PRICE}}, representing a 30-day decline of 10.44% and a 1-year change of -50.94%. | Metric | Details | | --- | --- | | Price | {{LIVE_PRICE}} | | 30-Day Change | -10.44% | | 1-Year Change | -50.94% | | Current Rank | {{LIVE_RANK}} | | Date | {{LIVE_DATE}} | ## Technical Analysis Trends Looking at historical data, HBAR has consistently faced resistance near the $0.10 level, a point reinforced by its 30-day high of $0.102507. Support levels have been tested repeatedly, with the 1-year low of $0.078421 serving as a critical psychological and technical floor. Should the price drop below this level, the next area of interest remains significantly lower, while a recovery above the $0.10 resistance would likely require substantial institutional volume, similar to patterns seen in larger networks like Solana or Cardano. ## Market Cap Scenarios Predicting future movement involves evaluating different market conditions. In a bear case scenario, persistent negative sentiment and lack of significant enterprise adoption could keep HBAR range-bound between $0.06 and $0.08. A base case assumes the current market neutrality continues, with HBAR trading between $0.08 and $0.12, tracking the average 30-day price of $0.096719. In a bull case, where network activity surges, the token could potentially retest previous highs, provided global market liquidity expands. ## Risk Factors Affecting Price The TokenRadar Risk Score of 7 highlights that HBAR is currently classified as high-risk. Key factors contributing to this include a volatility index of 16 and the fact that the token is trading significantly below its all-time high of $0.569229. Investors should account for the potential for token unlocks or dilution, which can often pressure price action in Layer 1 projects. ## Peer Comparison In the competitive landscape, Hedera often draws comparisons to Cardano and Solana. Unlike Solana, which has seen massive surges in retail volume, or Cardano, which maintains a large, loyal community, Hedera relies heavily on corporate pilot programs. This focus leads to different market dynamics where HBAR may exhibit lower correlation with typical "meme" or retail-driven crypto cycles, resulting in longer accumulation phases. ## FAQ ## FAQ - What is the historical context of HBAR? HBAR reached an all-time high of $0.569229 on September 15, 2021, and has since corrected significantly to its current price of {{LIVE_PRICE}}. - What determines the price of HBAR? Like most assets, price is driven by liquidity, adoption of the hashgraph network, and overall market sentiment toward the L1 sector. - Is Hedera a high-risk asset? With a TokenRadar Risk Score of 7, HBAR is categorized as high-risk, largely due to price volatility and its distance from historical highs. --- *Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. Always do your own research (DYOR).*
